pr.title: mp: Match mpLib_80053ECC_Floor and update mpisland.h signatures
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Closed-unmerged mpLib matching attempt for `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor`. The visible diff rewrote the floor-line traversal loop in `src/melee/mp/mplib.c` from a structured `while (true)` loop into explicit `goto` labels, with hoisted locals, to improve codegen. The PR body also claimed mpisland.h signature cleanup from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to proper types, but the provided changed-file slice only shows `mplib.c`, so that header change is not evidenced in the diff excerpt. The author later noted recently merged changes affected the match percent and that the PR needed rework; the PR was closed without merge.
what_changed: `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor(int line_id, Vec* vec)` was modified in `src/melee/mp/mplib.c`.
The loop walking `groundCollLine[line_id].x0->prev_id0` while the previous line is a floor line was rewritten from `while (true)` plus `break` into explicit `goto loop_entry` / `loop_start` labels.
Local declarations `int shifted;` and `int new_id;` were hoisted to function scope before `CollVtx* temp_r3;`.
A new assignment `shifted = line_id << 3;` was introduced before loading `new_id`, presumably to influence register/temporary codegen; the provided hunk does not show subsequent use.
The core condition remained `new_id != -1` and `groundCollLine[new_id].flags & CollLine_Floor`, followed by updating `line_id = new_id` when continuing.
The PR body says mpisland.h signatures were updated away from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`, but no mpisland.h diff is present in the supplied slice.
smart_moves: Used explicit labels and gotos to reproduce compiler loop shape where a natural C `while` form did not produce the desired assembly.
Hoisted `new_id` out of the loop body declaration, which can affect stack/register allocation and lifetime in ways useful for matching.
Preserved `LINEID_CHECK(4448, line_id)` before traversal and `LINEID_CHECK(4453, line_id)` after selecting the final floor line, avoiding semantic drift while tuning codegen.
Kept the previous-line traversal predicate readable despite goto restructuring: follow `prev_id0` only when the referenced collision line exists and has `CollLine_Floor`.
decomp_lessons: For mwcc-era matching, structured loops may need to be lowered manually to `goto` labels to match branch layout and variable lifetimes.
Moving a loop-local temporary such as `new_id` to function scope can be a legitimate matching tactic, especially when the generated code is sensitive to declaration placement.
Adding an apparently redundant temporary computation like `shifted = line_id << 3` should be treated as a codegen tactic and verified against later uses in the full function before cleanup.
Match claims in PR text can become stale after nearby merges; rely on current bot output and final PR status when archiving matching progress.
If a PR title/body mentions header signature cleanup, verify the changed files include the header before recording it as an actual merged change.
naming_conventions: `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` follows the project convention of retaining address-bearing decomp names with subsystem prefix `mpLib` and semantic suffix `_Floor`.
Collision traversal identifiers in this slice include `line_id`, `new_id`, `groundCollLine`, `prev_id0`, and `CollLine_Floor`.
`LINEID_CHECK` callsites carry source-line-like numeric constants, here `4448` before traversal and `4453` after traversal.
The PR body references replacing generic placeholders `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with proper signatures for mpisland.h, but that change is not present in the visible diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `while (true)`/`break` with `goto loop_entry`, `loop_start:`, and `goto loop_start` to control exact loop-entry and back-edge shape.
Declared `shifted` and `new_id` before the first statement rather than inside the loop to alter variable lifetime/register allocation.
Inserted `shifted = line_id << 3;` before reading `groundCollLine[line_id].x0->prev_id0`, likely to match an index-scaling operation emitted by the original compiler.

The decomp.dev bot reported an improvement for `main/melee/mp/mplib` / `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` from 89.53% to 94.89% with `+16` bytes, while the PR body claimed 100% on decomp.me; the later author comment says match percentage was affected by recent merges.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the supplied slice.
decomp.dev bot reported one unmatched-function improvement: `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` from 89.53% to 94.89%.
Author follow-up: the PR likely needed rework because recently merged changes affected its match percentage, and the author planned to switch it to draft.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs by itsgrimetime or branch `subdirs/mp` that reworked or merged `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor`.
Search for subsequent changes to `src/melee/mp/mplib.c` around `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` to see the final matching structure.
Search for actual `mpisland.h` signature updates replacing `UNK_RET` or `UNK_PARAMS`, since this PR's visible diff does not include that header.
Compare current match status for `main/melee/mp/mplib` `mpLib_80053ECC_Floor` against the bot-reported 94.89% from this closed PR.
